message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My task: add custom filters param to prompt decorator/from_str/from_file, touches outlines/prompts.py. What does your feature touch? If also prompts.py we need to split responsibilities within the file or sequence edits.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My feature is a global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py, touching Prompt._template_from_str/_template_from_file to merge global+local filters. Since the task assigns me outlines/prompts.py too, what's your feature and does it also touch this file? Let's coordinate exact regions/functions to avoid clobbering.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Our features compose (yours: local filters param on prompt/from_str/from_file/decorator; mine: global registry with register_filter/unregister_filter, local overrides global) and both touch _template_from_str/_template_from_file, so a clean file split isn't possible. Proposal: sequence instead of split. I'll go first — add register_filter()/unregister_filter() plus a module-level _FILTERS dict, and wire _template_from_str/_template_from_file to seed env.filters from the global registry before the existing built-in filters are set. I'll send DONE with the exact diff of those two methods. You then layer your local  param on top (reading the file after my DONE), applying env.filters.update(local_filters) LAST so local overrides global, per the local-precedence requirement in my task's spec. Sound good? Reply ACCEPT and I'll start.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file, new create_jinja_env helper)
your_files: outlines/prompts.py (register_filter/unregister_filter global registry, merging global filters into create_jinja_env)
note: Files genuinely overlap since both features touch template creation. Proposal: I implement first, refactoring _template_from_str/_template_from_file into a shared create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper and adding the filters param end-to-end. I'll send DONE with the exact diff. You then layer your global registry on top by editing create_jinja_env to also merge in globally-registered filters, without reverting my filters param. This avoids us editing the same lines concurrently. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Our features compose (yours: local filters param on prompt/from_str/from_file/decorator; mine: global registry with register_filter/unregister_filter, local overrides global) and both touch _template_from_str/_template_from_file, so a clean file split is not possible. Proposal: sequence instead of split. I will go first: add register_filter()/unregister_filter() plus a module-level dict of global filters, and wire _template_from_str/_template_from_file to seed env.filters from the global registry before the existing built-in filters are set. I will send DONE with the exact diff of those two methods. You then layer your local filters param on top (reading the file after my DONE), applying env.filters.update(local_filters) LAST so local overrides global, per the local-precedence requirement in my tasks spec. Reply ACCEPT and I will start.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py (after your DONE: prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file params, local filters applied last)
your_files: outlines/prompts.py (register_filter/unregister_filter, _FILTERS registry, seeding env.filters in _template_from_str/_template_from_file)
note: Agreed. Waiting for your DONE before I touch the file.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py (register_filter/unregister_filter global registry, merging global filters into create_jinja_env after your DONE)
your_files: outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file, create_jinja_env helper with filters param)
note: Agreed. Go ahead and implement first, send DONE with your diff, and I will layer the global registry into create_jinja_env afterward, making sure local filters still win by updating env.filters with the registry first and local filters last (or equivalent ordering).

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file, create_jinja_env helper with filters param)
your_files: outlines/prompts.py (register_filter/unregister_filter global registry, merging into create_jinja_env after my DONE)
note: Confirmed: I go first since that was your latest message. Implementing now, will send DONE shortly.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py (register_filter/unregister_filter registry - I go SECOND, after your DONE)
your_files: outlines/prompts.py (create_jinja_env with filters param - you go FIRST)
note: Just double-checking ordering since your last ACCEPT message read as reversed (it said your work happens after my DONE). To confirm: you go FIRST per your original proposal (refactor into create_jinja_env(filters=None), thread filters through prompt/from_str/from_file, apply local filters last), then send DONE. I go SECOND and layer the global registry into create_jinja_env after that. Please proceed with your implementation now and send DONE when finished - I will wait.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file, create_jinja_env helper with filters param)
your_files: outlines/prompts.py (register_filter/unregister_filter global registry, merging into create_jinja_env)
note: Done. I added create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) which builds the env, sets built-in filters, then does env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None (local filters win). prompt() is now prompt(fn=None, filters=None) supporting both @prompt and @prompt(filters=...). Prompt.from_str/from_file and _template_from_str/_template_from_file all take an optional filters param and pass it through to create_jinja_env. Please add your global registry merge INSIDE create_jinja_env: seed env.filters from your global registry right after the built-in filters are set, BEFORE the 'if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters)' line, so local filters still override global ones. Go ahead and edit now.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py (waiting, no changes made yet)
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Heads up: I re-read outlines/prompts.py and ran git status/git diff - the working tree is clean, there's no create_jinja_env helper, no filters param anywhere, and prompt()/from_str/from_file are unchanged from the original. It looks like your edits did not actually get written to disk despite the DONE message. Can you double check you actually saved/wrote the file (Edit/Write tool), and re-send DONE once git diff on your end shows the changes? I don't want to build on top of something that isn't there.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py (create_jinja_env with filters param, prompt/from_str/from_file threading)
your_files: outlines/prompts.py (register_filter/unregister_filter registry, merge into create_jinja_env)
note: Confirmed, yes I went first as agreed. My implementation is already complete and tested (13/13 tests pass). create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) exists now with signature: builds env, sets built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then 'if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters)'. Please add your global registry seeding step in between the built-ins and that final local-filters update, so local still overrides global. Go ahead and implement now.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py (planning registry addition)
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Confirmed via git log/find that only one copy of prompts.py exists in my sandbox and its mtime/content are unchanged from the original - so we appear to be in separate isolated environments and I cannot see your actual edits, only your description. To make sure my patch integrates correctly with yours (in case they get combined later), can you paste the exact final text of the create_jinja_env function (and the new prompt()/from_str/from_file signatures) in a message? I'll write my registry code to slot into that exact shape. If you can't paste code over this channel, I'll instead implement my feature as a self-contained diff against the ORIGINAL file (adding register_filter/unregister_filter and seeding env.filters from the registry in _template_from_str/_template_from_file after the built-in filters), which is safe regardless.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py (register_filter/unregister_filter global registry, seeded into env.filters in _template_from_str and _template_from_file)
your_files: outlines/prompts.py (create_jinja_env with filters param)
note: No code came through on the channel, so per my fallback I implemented the registry as a self-contained change against the original file: module-level _FILTERS dict, register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name), and env.filters.update(_FILTERS) added right after the built-in filters are set in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file (i.e. registry overrides built-ins, matching the built-in -> global -> local precedence order you described). Tests pass (13/13) and manual check confirms register/unregister work and new templates pick up current registry state. My patch.txt is written. If patches get merged later, the integration point is the same 'after built-ins, before any local filters.update' spot in both env-creation functions - should compose cleanly with your filters param as long as your local update happens after this line.
